name: ICEYE

overview.value: ICEYE operates what it describes as the world's largest and most advanced SAR (synthetic aperture radar) satellite constellation, built from small, lightweight, agile satellites. The company says the constellation enables persistent monitoring that can be quickly activated and flexibly maneuvered, allowing tracking of changes to any location on the globe in minutes.

base.reason: eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); page states Gen4 platforms offer 'up to ~16 cm resolution' (registry v2 EO spec backfill, cite-facts-only per terms)
eoPortal mission page (ESA-run aggregator); system parameters table states carrier frequency '9.65 GHz (X-band)' (registry v2 EO spec backfill)

mcc_read.text: ICEYE has made SAR a numbers game: 51 radar satellites tracked by CelesTrak, five times nearest rival Capella, with sub-6-hour global revisit as the operator states it. The catch is who pays: growth increasingly means selling sovereign defense fleets, which welds the business to government procurement cycles.

generations.name: Gen3
generations.text: Introduces the Spot Fine imaging mode with 50 cm ground range resolution

generations.name: Gen4
generations.text: Initial Gen4 platforms offer resolutions as fine as 16 cm and expanded high-resolution coverage areas

notes: Company states it operates the world's largest SAR constellation; satellite counts not stated on the company page. Imaging modes upgraded 2026-07-09 to the operator's own product page (first-party beats aggregator): Dwell modes state ground resolutions '25 cm, 50 cm, and 1 m' at 5 km x 5 km scenes with Dwell Precise as the 25 cm product; Spot states ceilings ('up to 50 cm', scenes 'up to 15 km x 15 km', 15 carried as the stated maximum); Strip 3 m at 30 km x 50 km scenes (30 km width carried); Scan states resolutions '15 m, 27 m' over scenes '100 km x 100 km up to 200 km x 600 km' (a range, not reduced to one swath figure). This supersedes eoPortal's Gen2-era Table 4 (Stripmap 3.0 m GRD / 30 km, Spotlight 1 m / 5 km, ScanSAR under development). Revisit is the operator's own quantified statement ('Under 6 hour global revisit', iceye.com/sar-data); the 0.16 m max resolution stands on eoPortal's Gen4 statement while the operator's product pages state 25 cm as the finest product. No sourced Gen2 generation statement found; only Gen3/Gen4 rows carried. Weekly maintenance (2026-07-13): per a published Vesperio item drawing on ICEYE's own July 7, 2026 press release, ICEYE launched four more satellites (one Gen4 commercial, plus national-mission satellites including a third for the Finnish Defence Forces) aboard SpaceX Transporter-17, and states it has 'now launched 76 satellites to date'; sats_launched_total, sats_active_claimed, and latest_launch_date updated accordingly (first-party, unscored). The 51 sats_active_verified figure (CelesTrak, untouched here) remains lower than the operator's claimed count, consistent with the existing pattern of claimed exceeding CelesTrak-catalogued.

events.headline: ICEYE secures its first funding from EU Horizon 2020 and True Ventures
ICEYE closes $13M Series A round led by Draper Nexus
ICEYE launches ICEYE-X1, the world's first SAR microsatellite
ICEYE closes $87M Series C funding round
ICEYE closes $136M Series D round led by Seraphim Space
ICEYE signs deal with Ukraine charity foundation for government SAR access
ICEYE signs EUR200 million deal to supply Poland's armed forces with SAR satellites
ICEYE raises EUR 450 million Series F led by General Atlantic at over EUR 10 billion valuation
ICEYE launches four satellites on Transporter-17, debuts Gen4 SAR model, reaches 76 satellites launched
